raw_text:
Customer: I have 2 Duo devices experiencing issues and would like to swap them for new ones.

Support Agent: One device had a firmware update applied last week that resolved its connectivity issue. The second device is connected via cellular but not connecting to WiFi. Please check if the device is whitelisted on your network firewall and verify WiFi credentials. We want to exhaust all configuration options before considering hardware replacement.

ideal_output.problem_summary: Customer requested hardware replacement for two Duo devices experiencing connectivity issues
ideal_output.solution_summary: One device issue resolved via firmware update. Second device has WiFi connectivity issue — support advised checking network firewall whitelist, WiFi credentials, and IoT device restrictions before hardware swap
ideal_output.reasoning: The customer initially requested a hardware swap, but support investigation revealed one device was already fixed via firmware update. The remaining issue is WiFi connectivity (not hardware), as the device has cellular but no WiFi. Root cause is therefore WiFi/network config, which falls under Infrastructure Issue in the taxonomy. Severity is medium because one device is resolved and the other has a workaround (cellular).

raw_text:
Customer: MB77 both sensors are online now. You may close the ticket. Thanks.

Support Agent: Great, glad to hear it! Closing the ticket.

ideal_output.problem_summary: Sensor connectivity issue — sensors were offline (now resolved)
ideal_output.solution_summary: Sensors came back online; customer confirmed resolution and requested ticket closure
ideal_output.issue_category: Sensors & PLC Connectivity (PLCs, Kepware, wiring, signal ingestion)
ideal_output.reasoning: Customer confirmed sensors are online and closed the ticket, indicating the connectivity issue was resolved. Root cause is assumed to be a temporary outage as no specific cause was mentioned, mapped to Infrastructure Issue.

raw_text:
Customer: I need help adding activities to specific stop causes on specific lines at one site.

Support Agent: Sure, I can walk you through that. Go to the Activity Templates section, select your line, then assign stop causes to each activity type.

ideal_output.problem_summary: Customer needs help configuring activity templates and linking them to specific stop causes on a production line
ideal_output.solution_summary: Support guided customer through Activity Templates section to assign stop causes to activity types per line
ideal_output.reasoning: Customer needed guidance on a platform configuration task. Support provided step-by-step instructions. This is a low severity how-to request, mapped to User Error (as in 'User needed assistance').
